dependencies: Third-party ground and air transport for clinical trial and diagnostic testing supplies and specimens
Extensive network of third-party suppliers and vendors of services and products
Reagents, test kits, or instruments from manufacturers
risks: Disruptions to supply and services from export/import restrictions or embargoes
Adverse weather, natural disasters, public health crises
Termination of relationships with suppliers or vendors for failure to follow performance standards
Discontinuation or recalls of reagents, test kits, or instruments used in laboratory testing

regulatory_risks: Changes in government and third-party payer regulations, reimbursement, or coverage policies in the U.S. healthcare system
Impact of the U.S. Protecting Access to Medicare Act of 2014 (PAMA)
Enforcement of anti-fraud and abuse laws (False Claims Act, Anti-Kickback Statute, Stark Law)
Failure to comply with privacy and security laws (HIPAA, HITECH Act, GDPR, CCPA, CPRA)
Loss or suspension of licenses under CLIA, state laboratory regulations, and similar laws in other jurisdictions
FDA regulation of laboratory-developed tests (LDTs) and in vitro diagnostics
European Union In Vitro Diagnostics Regulation (IVDR) and Medical Device Regulation (EU MDR)
Compliance with occupational and workplace safety laws (OSHA, Needlestick Safety and Prevention Act)
Compliance with current good manufacturing practice (cGMP) regulations
Compliance with Animal Welfare Act (AWA) and similar laws in other jurisdictions
Changes in testing guidelines or recommendations by government agencies
Compliance with environmental, health and safety laws and regulations
Compliance with FCPA, UK Bribery Act, and other anti-corruption laws
Changes in tax laws and regulations
Pillar Two legislation from OECD base erosion and profit shifting initiative
Price transparency initiatives and surprise billing laws

sanction_risks: Compliance with trade sanction laws and regulations
Export/import restrictions or embargoes affecting supply chain
